title: ‘SNL’ recap: Best moments from Ryan Gosling hosting ‘Saturday Night Live’
author: Kevin Slane
bullets: Ryan Gosling hosted 'SNL' on March 7, showing his knack for humor in pre-tapes and live sketches.
Gorillaz performed on 'SNL,' playing 'Clint Eastwood' to mark the band's 25th anniversary and upcoming album.
Harry Styles, next week's host and musical guest, attended Gosling's monologue, leading to a humorous moment.

title: Crew member on sunken vessel off Cape Cod has died, officials identify him
author: Darin Zullo
bullets: Angel Nieves, 37, of New Bedford, died after the Yankee Rose capsized off Cape Cod, officials confirmed.
The search for a second missing crew member from the Yankee Rose vessel has been halted due to poor weather.
The capsized Yankee Rose was previously involved in a carbon monoxide incident on February 26, affecting eight people.

title: Patriots reportedly among teams interested in Packers’ leading WR in free agency
author: Conor Roche
bullets: Patriots showing interest in Packers WR Romeo Doubs as free agency approaches, among other potential pass-catchers.
Romeo Doubs, a 2022 fourth-round pick, recorded 724 yards and six touchdowns for the Packers last season.
Doubs is considered a top-five WR in free agency, projected for a 3-year, $49 million deal with $30 million guaranteed.

title: NH cold case solved 40 years after police found man’s skull in woods
author: Darin Zullo
bullets: Warren Kuchinsky's skull, found in Bristol, NH in 1986, was identified through genetic genealogy.
The DNA Doe Project confirmed Kuchinsky's identity using DNA testing from a surviving family member.
No foul play is suspected in Kuchinsky's death; case solved with the help of 40 volunteer genealogists.

title: Boston police issue scam warning for fake parking violations
author: Darin Zullo
bullets: Boston police warn of fake parking violation notices, falsely appearing as official Boston Municipal Court documents.
Scammers include false court details and a QR code that prompts recipients to pay, leading to potential fraud.
Victims are urged to verify parking notices through the city's website and report scams to the CrimeStoppers tip line.

title: Mansfield man charged with illegally possessing ammunition, prosecutors say
author: Morgan Rousseau
bullets: Michael Kennedy, 48, charged with unlawful possession of ammunition in Mansfield while on supervised release for prior convictions.
Investigators found ammunition, firearm parts, and a loaded pistol in Kennedy's home during a search by authorities.
If convicted, Kennedy faces up to 15 years in prison, supervised release, and a possible $250,000 fine.

title: Here are four under the radar Red Sox players standing out in spring training
author: Colin McCarthy
bullets: Braiden Ward stands out with 10 hits and 12 stolen bases in 12 spring training games for the Red Sox.
Max Ferguson leads the Red Sox with 7 RBIs this spring, showcasing positional versatility in the minor leagues.
Tayron Guerrero impresses with 6 relief innings, 6 strikeouts, and only 1 earned run in Red Sox spring training.

title: Once viewed as a rental, could Viktor Arvidsson be ideal ‘bridge’ forward for Bruins?
author: Conor Ryan
bullets: Viktor Arvidsson ranks second on the Bruins in 5-on-5 goals this season with 13.
The Bruins opted not to trade Arvidsson despite his expiring contract, valuing his contribution to the team.
Arvidsson's potential as a bridge forward depends on contract negotiations and the emergence of young talent.

title: 5 interior offensive linemen options for Patriots after Garrett Bradbury trade
author: Conor Roche
bullets: The Patriots will likely look to add a left guard after trading center Garrett Bradbury to the Bears.
Isaac Seumalo, a free agent from the Steelers, is considered the top available guard for the Patriots this offseason.
Potential draft prospect Emmanuel Pregnon from Oregon could be a target for the Patriots to bolster their offensive line.

title: He’s running the Boston Marathon for the ‘amazing’ Make-A-Wish children
author: Annie Jonas
bullets: Jason Perry, 48, from Stockton, California, is running the 2026 Boston Marathon to support Make-A-Wish children.
Perry raises funds and awareness for Make-A-Wish in Greater Massachusetts-Rhode Island, inspired by the children's resilience and hope.
He dedicates his marathon run to both past and present Wish children who bring joy despite their challenges.
